commands.review_summary:
The PR adds active-model provider checks before exposing the xAI `code_execution` and `x_search` lazy tools.

PR surface: Source +13. Total +13 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main exposes both xAI-billed tools from xAI credential availability without an active-provider gate, and the PR still leaves that path open when provider metadata is missing. I did not run a live multi-provider billing or tool-list reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(xai): gate server-side tools to xAI models only
target.body:
Closes #97621

## What Problem This Solves

xAI server-side tools (`code_execution`, `x_search`) were offered to agents running on non-xAI models whenever an xAI API key was present. A non-xAI model (e.g. GLM-5.2) calling `code_execution` would silently POST to `api.x.ai` and bill the xAI account — cross-provider billing with no indication to the user.

The tool factories `createLazyCodeExecutionTool` and `createLazyXSearchTool` gated on credential availability and config flags, but not on the active model's provider identity.

## Why This Change Was Made

Added an `activeModel?.provider` gate at the top of both lazy tool factories. When the active model's provider doesn't match `xai`, the factory returns `null` (tool unavailable) before any auth/config checks run. This mirrors the pattern used by other provider-specific tools in the codebase (e.g. Google's `code_execution` gate).

The gates are positioned before the config/auth checks as a fast-path: if the active model is from a different provider, there's no scenario where these tools should be offered.

## Evidence

### Source-level proof
- `PROVIDER_ID` is `"xai"` (defined in the same file)
- Both factories already receive an `activeModel` context — the fix just reads `provider` from it
- `ctx.activeModel?.provider !== PROVIDER_ID` → `return null` (no-op for xAI models, gate for others)

### Existing tool resolution
- Tools are lazily resolved per-turn, so the active model is always available
- Returning `null` from a lazy factory is the standard way to hide a tool (used by the config/auth checks already in these factories)

### Not tested
- Live tool resolution with multiple provider credentials configured (requires multi-provider gateway)

## Merge Risk

**Low**. The change:
- Adds a pre-condition gate at the top of both factories (no existing control flow affected)
- Only returns `null` (the standard "tool unavailable" signal)
- Non-xAI models that previously had these tools will simply not see them — no error, no disruption
- +13 lines across one file

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: krissding
